commit.message:
fix(tun): use async sleep to wait for TUN interface visibility before route setup (#1060)

* Initial plan

* fix(tun): wait for tun visibility using async sleep before adding routes

Fixes #1032 - intermittent TUN startup crash "tun interface not found".

After build_async() creates a new TUN device, the interface may not be
immediately visible via NetworkInterface::show(). This caused maybe_add_routes()
to call get_interface_by_name() -> None -> .expect() -> panic (SIGABRT).

Changes:
- Add Clone derive to TunConfig (config/internal/config.rs)
- Replace .expect("tun interface not found") with proper error return in
  maybe_add_routes (routes/mod.rs)
- Make new_internal an async associated function taking &TunConfig; add
  retry loop (up to 40x50ms = 2s) using tokio::time::sleep (not
  std::thread::sleep which blocks Tokio runtime) to wait for TUN
  interface visibility before calling maybe_add_routes (runner.rs)
- Update run_async to clone cfg and await new_internal inside tokio::spawn

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): address review comments - fix poll loop and surface NetworkInterface errors

- Restructure TUN visibility poll loop to avoid sleeping after the
  last attempt, eliminating false-negative window where interface
  becomes visible during the final sleep but still errors
- Capture NetworkInterface::show() errors and include the last error
  message in the Error::Operation return for better diagnostics
- Fix fmt/clippy issues in runner.rs

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): fix nightly fmt - adjust line break in format string

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

Connection keys (host, port, database, username, password) removed from
configuration.yaml - these were auto-imported to the UI integration and

commit.message:
feat: add refresh token auth and WebAuthn biometric login

- Replace 72-hour access tokens with 15-minute access + 30-day rolling
  refresh tokens for seamless session persistence
- Add POST /auth/refresh endpoint that issues new token pairs and
  revokes the old refresh token
- Add frontend axios interceptor that silently refreshes expired access
  tokens, queuing concurrent requests during refresh
- Implement WebAuthn/FIDO2 backend with go-webauthn library for
  Face ID, Touch ID, and fingerprint authentication
- Add registration and login ceremony endpoints under /auth/webauthn
- Add biometric login button to LoginPage with automatic detection
  of registered credentials and last-used username memory
- Add biometric credential management to SettingsPage (register,
  list, remove)
- Add RefreshToken and WebAuthnCredential database models
- Configure WEBAUTHN_RP_ID and WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN via environment variables
- Update Swagger types to include refreshToken in AuthResponse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: map https proxy scheme to http for httpx compatibility

When GGWP Proxy service returns protocol='https', the scraper was
building proxy URLs like https://ip:port. httpx interprets this as
SSL-to-proxy (TLS tunnel TO the proxy server itself), causing connection
failures for standard forward proxies that use plain HTTP CONNECT.

Map https -> http for proxy URLs (same as proxy.service.ts line 957),
so httpx correctly uses HTTP CONNECT tunneling to reach HTTPS targets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix PR #2 doc review follow-ups

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: optimize extract_statement_type regex and encoding in helpers-mysql (#2088)

* perf: optimize extract_statement_type regex and encoding

Remove trailing .* from QUERY_NAME_REGEX to avoid scanning the entire
SQL string after matching the keyword. Only the first capture group
(the SQL operation) is used — the .* forced O(n) cost for no benefit.

Skip utf8_encode when the string is already valid UTF-8, which is the
common case for strings from ActiveRecord/Trilogy. Fall back to
binary: true encoding only for non-UTF-8 or invalid byte sequences.

Benchmarked improvements (combined):
- Short queries (53c):  1.91µs → 833ns  (2.3×)
- Long JOINs (768c):    6.97µs → 1.13µs (6.2×)
- IN clauses (2,406c): 15.19µs → 886ns  (17.1×)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: fix rubocop offenses

- Use modifier unless for single-line body
- Use unary plus (+) instead of .dup for unfreezing strings
- Use single-quoted strings where interpolation is not needed
- Remove redundant .freeze on string literals (frozen_string_literal)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Ariel Valentin <arielvalentin@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: cache static span attributes in Trilogy instrumentation (#2089)

* perf: cache static attributes in Trilogy instrumentation

Cache base span attributes (db.system, net.peer.name, db.name, db.user,
peer_service) at connection initialization rather than rebuilding a new
hash on every query. Use Hash#dup from the frozen cached hash instead
of constructing from scratch each time.

Also cache database_name and database_user as instance variables, and
read Trilogy.attributes once per query() call instead of twice.

Benchmarked improvements:
- client_attributes (no sql):  864ns → 163ns (5.3×)
- client_attributes (with sql): 946ns → 235ns (4.0×)
- database_name: 318ns → 58ns (5.5×)
- database_user: 193ns → 59ns (3.3×)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: fix rubocop and formatting offenses in test file

- Align hash arguments per Layout/HashAlignment
- Remove trailing commas per Style/TrailingCommaInHashLiteral
- Use single-quoted strings per Style/StringLiterals

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: inline attribute setup per review feedback

- Inline populate_base_attributes into initialize
- Merge build_base_attributes into _build_otel_base_attributes
- Remove database_name and database_user accessor methods
- Use local variable for database_user in builder (no ivar needed)
- Keep @_otel_database_name as ivar (needed by query() for span naming)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add Tdarr HEVC compression configuration guide

Add TDARR.md with complete setup guide for compressing x264 TV/movie
libraries to HEVC (x265) using CPU-based libx265 encoding. Covers:
- Tdarr CRUDDB API commands (docker exec based, no port mapping)
- Library creation for TV, Anime TV, Indian TV (+ future movie libs)
- Flow builder pattern with HDR/SDR branching
- Per-library CRF via library variables (18 for TV/anime, 20 for Indian)
- Sonarr/Radarr notification integration
- FFmpeg command reference for manual testing

Update README.md to reference TDARR.md and add tdarr to Starr services.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add multi-tenant data models, migrations, controllers, factories, and API routes

- Add 18 migrations: plans, tenants, features, plan_features, tenant_subscriptions,
  products, product_categories, product_category_product, customers, invoices,
  invoice_items, payments, stock_movements, orders, order_items, webshop_settings,
  setup_progress; alter users table with tenant_id/role/is_active/last_login_at

- Add 13 controllers with tenant-scoped data isolation
- Replace routes/api.php with full resource routes under auth:sanctum middleware
- Add 5 factories (Plan, Tenant, Product, Customer, Invoice); update UserFactory

commit.message:
fix: improve OpenAI prompt to enforce proportional nutritional scaling

The previous prompt instructed the model to return values 'adjusted for
quantity' but did not demonstrate HOW to scale. This caused gpt-4o-mini
to return approximate 100g values regardless of the actual quantity.

Changes:
- Add explicit 4-step proportional calculation methodology
- Standardize all TACO reference values to per-100g basis
- Add worked example showing 180g oats scaling (factor 1.8)
- Expand reference table with common foods (oats, sweet potato, pasta)
- Add safeguard rule: values MUST be greater than 100g base when qty > 100g
- Add final rule preventing 100g values for different quantities

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
